page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-metal is a bare-metal server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this configuration delivers 288 vCPUs across 144 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system features a 4.2 GHz clock speed and a substantial L3 cache of 504 MB. It is equipped with 1080.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 3.75 GB per core. No local storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 10%. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves exceptional read-only memory bandwidth, exceeding 1087 GB/sec for large uncached blocks and 23040 GB/sec for cached small blocks. Cryptographic and compression capabilities are highly performant, as evidenced by top-tier results in Passmark CPU encryption and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, image processing and small-model LLM text generation represent performance bottlenecks, with the latter falling into the bottom 10% of tested systems.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-288-metal offers high compute density and massive memory bandwidth on a bare-metal platform, making it highly efficient for workloads that can fully saturate its 288 dedicated vCPUs. The primary architectural tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage solutions. Additionally, the lack of dedicated GPU hardware limits its efficiency for continuous deep learning training, though it maintains strong performance for medium and large LLM inference prompt processing.

This server is highly suited for compute-intensive enterprise workloads, including large-scale database operations, high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded simulation tasks. It is also well-suited for medium-to-large LLM inference prompt processing where GPU acceleration is not strictly required.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-metal is a bare-metal server configured with 288 dedicated vCPUs from Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors running at 4.2 GHz, paired with 1080.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. This instance delivers top-tier multi-core compute and memory bandwidth performance, excelling in database operations, cryptography, and multi-threaded decompression. However, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, and exhibits lower performance in small-model LLM text generation. The c4-standard-288-metal is highly efficient for compute-dense enterprise workloads, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 288 vCPUs, 1080 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier multi-core compute and memory bandwidth.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform delivering 288 dedicated vCPUs and exceptional DDR5 memory bandwidth for compute-intensive enterprise workloads.

1080 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory with 504 MB L3 cache
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
